action: PERFORM CARBON RESISTANCE CHECK ON ALL CYLINDERS. CYLINDERS #1 AND #3 PASSED. CYLINDERS #2 AND #4 FAILED. CYLINDER #2, EXHAUST VALVE REMOVED AND EXHAUST GUIDES WERE REAMED. THE VALVE WAS REINSTALLED AND OPERATED SMOOTHLY. (CONT)

problem: CABIN HEAT VALVE BROKEN.
action: INSTALLED NEW VALVE & BOX ASSY. RIGGED VALVE CABLE, OPS CK GOOD.

problem: ENGINE RAN ROUGH A COUPLE TIMES DURING RUN UP.
action: REVIEWED FLIGHT DATA, FOUND CYL #4 EGT TO DROP. REAMED CYL #4 EXHAUST VALVE. CK'ED CLEARANCES, CK'ED GOOD. REPLACED CYL #4 EGT PROBE, PERFORMED GROUN RUN UP, OPS & LEAK CK'ED GOOD. SEE W/O #1171201 FOR MX FLIGHT.

problem: EXHAUST VALVE REAMING REQUIRED

problem: 10 MINS INTO CRUISE, EXPERIENCED MOMENTARY POWER LOSS FROM 2500 TO 2300 RPM.
action: REMOVED ROCKER COVERS, EXHAUST ROCKER ARMS & EXHAUST VALVE SPRINGS & KEEPERS. REAMED EXHAUST ALL 4 CYL GUIDES & CLEANED. INSTALLED VALVES, SPRINGS & KEEPERS, ROCKER ARMS. BLED LIFTERS. DRY TAPPET CLEARANCE CHECKS GOOD. INSTALLED ROCKER COVERS.

problem: ENGINE STARTED TO SHAKE SHORTLY AFTER START UP.
action: REAMED ALL EXHAUST VALVE GUIDES. DRY TAPPET CLEARANCE IN LIMITS. SERVICED ALL SPARK PLUGS. RAN ENGINE, OPS CK GOOD. LEAK CK GOOD.

problem: CARBON RESISTANCE CHECK
action: PERFORMED CARBON RESISTANCE CHECK IAW LYCOMING 51 1425A. FOUND CYLINDER 4 EXHAUST VALVE STICKING. REAMED #4 EXHAUST VALVE GUIDE. BORESCOPE INSPECTION OF CYLINDER OK. REASSEMBLED CYLINDER 2 AND 4 VALVETRAIN. CYLINDER 1,2,3 EXHAUST VALVE GUIDES OK.

problem: ENGINE RAN ROUGH ON LAST FLIGHT PILOT REPORTS ENGINE OVERSPEED
action: SEE PD 13 REPAIR CYLINDER 4 EXHAUST VALVE STICKING.

problem: FUEL STRAINER VALVE LEAKING.
action: REMOVED & REPLACED VALVE, LEAK CK GOOD.

problem: ON COLD ENGINE START UP, OIL TEMP @ 22* F, ENGINE WAS COLD ON #2 CYLINDER BUT RAN FINE ON OTHER 3 CYLINDERS. PRE-HEATED ENGINE TO 38* OIL TEMP & ENGINE RAN SMOOTH ON ALL 4. SUSPECT STICKING VALVE ON #2 CYLINDER.
action: REAMED #2 EXHAUST VALVE. WORK IAW LYCOMING DIRECT DRIVE OVERHAUL MANUAL. RAN ENGINE, OPS & LEAK CK GOOD.

problem: "FIRST START OF THE DAY, ENGINE IDLE ROUGH BELOW 1000 RPM. AFTER FLIGHT I RECHECKED IDLE AND IT WAS NORMAL. WRITTEN UP BECAUSE I  WAS WORRIED ABOUT POSSIBLE ENGINE "MORNING SICKNESS". PILOT REPORT.
action: MORNING SICKNESS IS A COLLOQUIAL TERM TO DESCRIBE CARBON BUILD UP ON THE EXHAUST VALVE. PERFORMED CARBON RESISTANCE CHECK PER UND SB22. NO DEFECTS NOTED. RAN ENGINE, FOUND FUEL SERVO NEEDS SEASONAL ADJUSTMENT. MADE SEASONAL ADJUSTMENT TO FUEL SETTINGS

problem: PILOT STATED: DID NOT NOTICE ANYTHING ABNORMAL DURING RUN-UP, BUT ON INITIAL CLIMB OUT AFTER TAKEOFF WE HAD VERY MINIMAL CLIMB PERFORMANCE. ABOUT 250 FPM AT VY. LEVELED OF AT 3500' AND HAD THROTTLE AT FULL AND RPMS WERE MAXING OUT AT 2350.
action: PERFORMED VALVE GUIDE RESISTANCE CHECK ON CYL #3 EXHAUST VALVE; GOOD. RE-INSTALLED UPPER SPARK PLUGS.

problem: R/H FUEL CAP CK VALVE MISSING.
action: INSTALLED NEW CHECKLIST VALVE ON FUEL CAP.

problem: L/H ENGINE SNIFFLE VALVE BARB BROKEN
action: REPLACED VALVE WITH NEW AND TRIMMED HOSE WHERE VALVE STEM STILL IN. NO FURTHER DEFECTS NOTED.

problem: ENGINE/ROTOR OVERSPEED IN FLARE PORTION OF AUTO ROTATION. UNSURE WHAT NUMBE ENGINE REACHED, ROTOR RPM APPEARED TO HIT ~113%.
action: REAMED #6 EXHAUST VALVE GUIDE. INSTALLED NEW ROD BOLTS & NUTS IN RODS.

Leak down on cylinders #1 - 78/80, #2 - 75/80, #3 - 77/80 cold. Inspected piston tops 1, 2 and 3; no valve strikes noted.

problem: Pilot stated: "Cylinder 4 EGT dropped and CHT dropped to 160 degrees."
